### Post by jenkinbr on 2009-03-20
I set up an alias for 'icanhazcheezburger'

alias makecheezburger='nano ~/bin/cheezburger'
alias icanhazcheezburger='sudo apt-get install `cheezburger`'

Oviously, the cheezburger script simply echos a list of packages to install.

Now, for the trophy...MINE!

### Post by jenkinbr on 2009-05-11
Look what I found!!!

[[size=5]**[COLOR="DarkRed"]_Microsoft has a majority market share_[/COLOR]**[/size]](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+bug/1)

Microsoft has a majority market share in the new desktop PC marketplace.
This is a bug, which Ubuntu is designed to fix.

Non-free software is holding back innovation in the IT industry, restricting access to IT to a small part of the world's population and limiting the ability of software developers to reach their full potential, globally. This bug is widely evident in the PC industry.

Steps to repeat:

1. Visit a local PC store.

What happens:
2. Observe that a majority of PCs for sale have non-free software pre-installed.
3. Observe very few PCs with Ubuntu and free software pre-installed.

What should happen:
1. A majority of the PCs for sale should include only free software like Ubuntu.
2. Ubuntu should be marketed in a way such that its amazing features and benefits would be apparent and known by all.
3. The system shall become more and more user friendly as time passes.

The last time I checked the Australian emergency number 000 doesn't do much here, unless you put in a few extra digits that get you through to a long-distance operator in another country. I might be mistaken but 999 & 911 from a landline result in a recorded message telling you the correct number for NZ (111). Test at your own peril: I don't want to be blamed for a fine for misuse of the emergency numbers!

### Post by The Toxic Mite on 2009-06-14
> **Wikipedia]The **Möbius strip** or **Möbius band** (pronounced [URL="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English"]/&#712 said:**
>  or /&#712;mo&#650;bi&#601;s/ in English, IPA: [[&#712;mø&#720;bi&#650;s]]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA") in German) (alternatively written Mobius or Moebius in English) is a [surface]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surface") with [highlight]only one side[/highlight] and only one [boundary component]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boundary_component"). The Möbius strip has the mathematical property of being [non-orientable]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orientability"). It is also a [ruled surface]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ruled_surface"). It was discovered independently by the [German]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Germany") [mathematicians]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mathematician") [August Ferdinand Möbius]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/August_Ferdinand_M%C3%B6bius") and [Johann Benedict Listing]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Johann_Benedict_Listing") in 1858.

[IMG]http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d9/M%C3%B6bius_strip.jpg/250px-M%C3%B6bius_strip.jpg[/IMG] 
"If an [ant]("http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ant") were to crawl along the length of this strip, it would return to its starting point having traversed [highlight]both sides[/highlight] of the strip, without ever crossing an edge."

The image caption, for some reason, contradicts the fact that the Möbius strip only has one side.

Therefore, jenkinbr gets to keep the trophy.

### Post by GepettoBR on 2009-06-14
> **The Toxic Mite said:**
> Ahem!

Isn't the trophy supposed to stay with Jenkinbr? He said no-one will take it unless they can prove that a Möbius strip has more than one side.

I proved that the Möbius strip has **only** one side. Therefore, the trophy stays with jenkinbr

Jenny doesn't have the power to decide such things, especially if her decision is to prove something that can't be proven. The definition of a Möbius strip requires that it have only one side. If you can prove that any given surface has more than one side, you have automatically proven that it is not a Möbius strip as well.

It's much like saying "I define an imaginary green creature, the gabeeplebop, as such a creature that all gabeeplebops are necessarily green. Prove me that not all gabeeplebops are green."

That said, I have the trophy now.

### Post by jenkinbr on 2009-06-19
I came across a house.
I knocked, but nobody answered.
I opened the door.
I went in, but it was dark.
I shut the door, and waited till my eyes ajusted.
I saw a light way down at the end of a long hallway.
I followed the hallway.
When I got to the end, it opened to a small dirt road in the woods.
I walked down the road.
As I was walking down the road, I noticed a sign at the start of a narrow trail pointing down it.
I read the sign. It said "Shortcut to town."
I followed the trail though the woods.
As I was walking through the woods, I came across a house.
I knocked, but nobody answered.
I opened the door.
I went in, but it was dark.
I shut the door, and waited till my eyes ajusted.
I saw a light way down at the end of a long hallway.
I followed the hallway.
When I got to the end, it opened to a small dirt road in the woods.
I walked down the road.
As I was walking down the road, I noticed a sign at the start of a narrow trail pointing down it.
I read the sign. It said "Shortcut to town."
I followed the trail though the woods.
As I was walking through the woods, ...........

Takes trophy while everyone follows the story....

### Post by aesis05401 on 2009-08-02
> **justinmiller87 said:**
> No, I need that for collateral. Give it back.

Heya,

Disclosure: I did not come up with the flip idea - it has been around since unicode support became widespread.  I bet you can figure it out pretty easily now.  

To actually see the trick:

1. Hover over my post # in the upper right hand corner of the upside down post.
2. Note the p= value displayed in your browser status bar - in this case it is 7719446 - this is UF's internal post ID.  
3. Click View/Page Source
4. ctrl+f search for 7719446 - this should take you to the open tag for my comment.
5. The <!-- message --> part of this comment is located about 2 pages down from opening tag, and should contain a single line of unicode characters. 

If you plug the unicode values into a lookup table you will see that we are not actually writing anything upside down - we are using characters that have been created in unicode for whatever reason - and there are a ton of them out there.  I think most flipping is done with extended latin.  

For instance - the 477 code that appears a couple times is pretty cool on its own:[http://www.eki.ee/letter/chardata.cgi?dcode=477]("http://www.eki.ee/letter/chardata.cgi?dcode=477")

Just a different type of l33t5p34k.  I now release this trophy to its fate.

### Post by jeffski on 2009-09-05
The wierdest thing happened to me today...

i was reading a forum (as you do) when i noticed some rather odd text, it went a bit like this "*Zalgo*. He w&#1161;&#821;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#839;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#831;&#831;&#831;&#789;&#794;&#789;&#794;&#865; &#786;&#787;&#788;&#789;&#794;ho Waits Behind &#1161;&#821;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#839;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#831;&#831;&#831;&#789;&#794;&#789;&#794;&#865; &#786;&#787;&#788;&#789;&#794;The Wall. [B]..."

[/B]Well i have never seen anything like it befoore in my life, so i start investigating.
Who or what is zalgo and how does he type like that?

Now i never did find out much about this subject, only this...

Little information has been gathered so far. All that seek the true origin of &#1161;&#1161; &#821;&#801;&#802;&#802;&#795;&#795;&#795;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787; Z&#1161;&#1161;&#801;&#802;&#801;&#802;&#795;&#795;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#1161;A&#1161;L&#1161;G&#1161;&#1161;&#821;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#790;&#791;&#792;&#793;&#796;&#797;&#798;&#799;&#800;&#839;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#794;&#789;&#794;&#1161; &#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#778;&#779;&#780;&#781;&#782;&#783;&#784;&#785;&#786;&#787;&#788;&#831;&#831;&#831;&#794;&#789;&#789;&#794;&#789;&#794;&#865; &#865;&#1161;&#1161; &#787;&#788;&#831;&#831;&#831;&#789;&#794;&#1769;&#65532;&#9674; have experienced [spontaneous computer errors]("http://encyclopediadramatica.com/Windows") and power outages.   

Thats when my monitors power supply went POP, it took me 2 hours of rummaging to find another one lol I'm sure it's just a coincidence so i started my search for zalgo again then POP screen goes blank again...

Now the really funny thing is this. my screen has a little menu, many do. when i turned it off at the socket then on again it usually comes up with "Auto tuning" or "no input" if the pc is off, but this time the text was all garbled! like the text on the web pages i had been reading.

I tested the power supply/transformer and the votage was there so i unscrewed my monitor and found one of the capacitors was loose. I soldered it back and it seems fine now. Damn cheap tiwanese lead free solder.

### Post by lisati on 2009-11-16
/me brushes up on long-forgotten IBM MVS/370 JCL and submits the following:
```
//trophy exec pgm=iefbr14
```
Then /me tries to port IEFBR14 to Linux.



(For the curious: IEFBR14 is a "do nothing" program on some IBM mainframes that lets you create or delete files using only the scripting language for batch jobs.)

//Opens trophy and calculates the SHA-1 hash for it
$trophy=sha1(file_get_contents('./trophy'));

file_put_contents('./trophy',$trophy);

echo "Good luck with getting your trophy back";

### Post by CompyTheInsane on 2009-11-17
It turns out that you grabbed a file named 'trophy back' instead of the hashed 'trophy'. Nice try
```

~$ mv ./trophy /media/flashdrive
~$ sudo umount /media/flashdrive

```
//Removes flashdrive and runs away

### Post by CompyTheInsane on 2010-04-11
```

sudo -i
mv trophy /home/compugeek32
chown compugeek32:compugeek32 /home/compugeek32/trophy
sudo -u compugeek32 cp /home/compugeek32/trophy /media/flashdrive
umount /media/flashdrive
make clean
exit

```
*Removes flashdrive and walks away*

### Post by JayRobert on 2010-05-08
Ole is a farmer in Minnesota . He is in need of a  new milk cow and hears about a nice one for sale over in Nordakota
(that   would be North Dakota for you non-Scandahoovians out there).

He  drives to  Nordakota, finds the farm and looks at the cow. He reaches under to see  if the  cow gives milk.

When he grabs a teat and pulls...the cow farts.  Surprised, Ole looks at the farmer who's selling the cow, then reaches  under to  try again. He grabs another teat, pulls, and the cow farts again. Milk  does come  out however, so after some discussion with the cow's current owner, Ole  decides  to buy the cow.

When he gets back to Minnesota , he calls over  his  neighbor, Sven, and says, 'Hey, Sven, come and look at dis ere new cow I  yust  bought. Pull her teat, and see vat happens.'

Sven reaches under,  pulls  the teat...the cow farts.

Sven looks at Ole and says, 'You bought  dis  here cow in Nordakota, didn't yah?'

Ole is very surprised since  he hadn't  told Sven about his trip. Ole replies, 'Yah, dats right. But how did yah   know?'

Sven says, '(something kind of naughty that I can't repeat in public with a clear conscience)'

(And the trophy is no longer in sqrooup's possession once I tell him the punchline.)

### Post by JayRobert on 2010-05-15
I doubt any of you would notice me walking quickly up the street from takisan's place with the trophy hidden beneath my topcoat; and if you did happen to look, the wispy, bedraggled gray hair and unkempt beard flapping maniacally in the wind, combined with the low growling singsong voice muttering "Turn it into the purple bird from that inkblot test, did you, Dr. Jetsam?  We'll see about that!!" would probably be enough to make you avert your eyes.

Back home, I apply myself in the shop, in short order expertly remaking the trophy into a magnificent specimen of award hardware; then, I place it ever so gently on the mantel.  The brand-new merino wool felt that I just attached to the base pleasantly muffles the solid  thunk as it comes to rest on the pristine white marble, the glimmering red and yellow reflections of the crackling fire in the hearth dancing merrily across the gleaming metal whilst I settle into my big comfortable leather recliner to continue reading the entries under the letter K in my Oxford English Dictionary.  "Ah, how I love weekends!" I sigh, skipping lightly past 'kazoo,' 'kea,' 'keach,' 'keach-cup,' 'kead,' 'keak,' and 'keal,' with the sweet anticipation of plunging again into the depths of 'keel' rapidly approaching, to be followed shortly by 'keen' and 'keep'!

"No one will ever get this trophy from me again," I whisper to myself.  My darling white Himalayan cat purrs contentedly on my lap.  "That's right, Jezebel...no one!" I say to her, scritching her behind her left ear, just the way she likes it.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-05-19
...and I'll take that, returning to the scene of the crime with an odd ointment.  It smells nice, but the tube is used.  I leave that in its place, and once again...

I have a pot 2P in.

...Finished with my duty, I toss it to the seas...

This time, before sinking, there's a rumble beneath the water.  

...There was a feeding.  Whatever it is is gone.  I don't think the trophy went down the right tube.  I think it wanted a meal, and got a blockage of the air supply instead.  Now it can live, but not dive.

Wounded whale, come back...


...too late.  It's gone to sea.  

Could be worse.  It only means one thing when they go for ground. 

I'm all wet.  It's cold.

I don't approve of that trophy.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-05-19
Rescues Moby from it, then tosses it in Ahab's general inadvertantly sinking his ship. 

"Shoulda built it out of brick," he says.  "Don't they ever learn?"

Rows through the wreckage, making sure Ishmail gets a plank big enough to walk on.  Turns...  oh... tragedy... another bunch of whale killers sinking with the seas...  Is that a fin I see?  


>SPLOOOOOOOOOSH<

...No doubt that WAS a fin.  

No it's morte like a cloud of RED RED WATER GOO.

I'm swimming right in the middle of it... better get back in my boat.  Oh... good think I'm small enough to fit in the LAST PLACE TROPHY, made of steel and finely honed with little oars on the side and most importantly of all, compartments...

Hoist the mizzenmast, tacken my jib, fly the ef yer mamma whaler daddy and I'm off, jibbering like a sardine in a fishbowl, not a can...

They look at me like I'm nuts... they just can't listen fast enough.

Sink or swim sailors!

SINK OR SWIM!!!

Carried away by the winds... the trophy takes on water and sinks like a stone three hours later.  

That's OK.

Tahiti is lovely this time of year, and I'm just outside the safety buoy.

Dead ahead: CLUB MED!!!

...And I take taki's copy of the original Helter Skelter on vinyl and drop it in the dumpster behind the headquarters of the sattelite radio corp. that went under after losing in the marketplace to XM. Large rats appear, enter the dumpster one by one.  The noises are gutteral, nibbling, sickening.

There's no longer any vinyl in evidence.  The smell is nauseating.

### Post by jetsam on 2010-06-14
If you took a plater cast of the hollow left in the box an iMac came in, you'd have an iMac-Void Made Tangible.

If you then, stuck the iMac-Void Made Tangible in dampened sand and burned away the plaster somehow, you'd have a Hole Where The iMac Once Resided.

If you then filled the hole with molten steel, you'd have a Negatronic iMac Made of Steel...  

If you then placed your Negatronic iMac Made of Steel on a vacuum table for forming plastic, you could make any number of Plastic iMac Jello Mo(u)lds...

How many Plastic iMac Jello Mo(u)lds would you make?

### Post by CompyTheInsane on 2010-07-16
The point is that he can't get my trophy. It's mine.
*Inserts flashdrive*
```

$ sudo -i
[sudo] password for compy:
$ cd /home/compy
$ mkdir /media/compydrive
$ mount /dev/sdb1 /media/compydrive
$ chown compy:compy ./trophy
$ cp ./trophy /media/compydrive
$ umount /media/compydrive
$ exit
$

```
*Takes flashdrive and teleports back home*

### Post by CJ Master on 2010-07-27
It is imperative that I give you the following information, which The current trophy holder wants concealed from the public. Here's how this letter works: I'll offer ideas and a theory to explain things. You bring your own experiences to bear on the matter of The current trophy holder's horny scribblings, supplementing them where necessary with information from this letter. Together we will honor our nation's glorious mosaic of cultures and ethnicities. Although he babbles on and on about colonialism, The current trophy holder has no more conception of it than any other savage, oleaginous publisher of hate literature. I wonder if he really believes the things he says. He knows they're not true, doesn't he? As you ponder the answer to that question, consider that he enjoys pondering new ways to pursue a pretentious agenda under the guise of false concern for the environment, poverty, civil rights, or whatever. That's probably obvious to a blind man on a galloping horse. Nevertheless, I suspect that few people reading this letter are aware that there is no honor in The current trophy holder's publications. Okay, that was a facetious statement. This one is not: A great many of us don't want The current trophy holder to stifle the voices of those who are simply seeking to be heard. Still, we feel a prodigious societal pressure to smile, to be nice, and not to object to his mealymouthed views.

To most people, the list of The current trophy holder's stubborn, macabre biases reads like a comic strip but his maneuvers are actually taken seriously by his confidants. If I am correct that The current trophy holder's attitude is honestly, "You don't agree with me; therefore, you must be a blockish firebrand", then by writing this letter, I am indeed sticking my head far above the parapet. The big danger is that The current trophy holder will retaliate against me. He'll most likely try to force me to go crazy although another possibility is that to The current trophy holder's mind, devious radicals are inherently good, sensitive, creative, and inoffensive. So that means that the health effects of secondhand smoke are negligible, right? No, not right. The truth is that The current trophy holder has long wanted to prevent anyone from stating publicly that his presence makes people nervous, anxious, fearful, and angry. Why do I bring that up? Because by studying his repression of ideas in its extreme, unambiguous form one may more clearly understand why we must understand that there is something patently malodorous in the notion that The current trophy holder is a moral exemplar. And we must formulate that understanding into as clear and cogent a message as possible.

The current trophy holder maintains that he would never dream of jumping on everything that is written, said, or even implied and label it as either intrusive or truculent. Even if this were so, The current trophy holder would still be picayunish. But I can guarantee the readers of this letter that I know some heinous, splenetic cockalorums who actually believe that The current trophy holder can convince criminals to fill out an application form before committing a crime. Incredible? Those same people have told me that public opinion is a reliable indicator of what's true and what isn't. With such people roaming about, it should come as no surprise to you that The current trophy holder keeps saying that he can succeed without trying. This is exemplary of the nonsensical rhetoric and scaremongering that typifies the language of improvident, abominable insurrectionists and other bookish buffoons. As I have tried to show in this letter, The current trophy holder's loquacity and volubility of tongue serve only to break us up into a set of quarreling, wrangling, squabbling factions. As long as you remember that, we may yet be able to take the lemons that The current trophy holder is handing us and make lemonade.

---

### Post by CJ Master on 2010-08-16
In my last letter, I claimed that the PREVIOUS trophy owner wants to drain our hope and enthusiasm, even though, for most people, this desire is neither necessary nor instinctive, and that claim is even more true now. The nitty-gritty of what I'm about to write is this: He is thoroughly gung-ho about mercantalism because he lacks more pressing soapbox issues. It's time that a few facts had a chance to slip through the fusillade of hype. That's pretty transparent. What's not so transparent is the answer to the following question: Has he ever considered what would happen if a small fraction of his time spent trying to befuddle the public and make sin seem like merely a sophisticated fashion was instead spent on something productive? A clue might be that I have some advice for him. He should keep his mouth shut until he stops being such an illiterate wisenheimer and starts being at least one of informative, agreeable, creative, or entertaining.

The PREVIOUS trophy owner maintains that anyone who disagrees with him is a potential terrorist. This is hardly the case. Rather, there is growing evidence that says, to the contrary, that this is not Nazi Germany or Soviet Russia, where the state would be eager to deny the obvious. Not yet, at least. But he wants to control every aspect of our lives. The PREVIOUS trophy owner wants us to rise, fall asleep, work, and live at the beat of a drum. Then, once we're molded into a uniform mass, we'll be incapable of seeing that the PREVIOUS trophy owner maintains control over his factotums using intense peer pressure, manipulation of their guilt feelings, attacks on their sexuality, and fear. (The merits of the PREVIOUS trophy owner's opinions won't be discussed here because they lack merit.)

So long as the devastating inequities that characterize our society persist, the PREVIOUS trophy owner's hirelings will be unable to deny that he fully intends to blitz media outlets with faxes and newsletters that highlight the good points of his brain-damaged canards. But that's not enough, not for him. The PREVIOUS trophy owner will additionally impugn the patriotism of his adversaries, which is why I believe that he uses big words like "microcrystallography" to make himself sound important. For that matter, benevolent Nature has equipped another puny creature, the skunk, with a means of making itself seem important, too. Although the PREVIOUS trophy owner's pleas may reek like a skunk, the PREVIOUS trophy owner's self-indulgent put-downs rifle, pillage, plunder, and loot. News of this deviousness must spread like wildfire if we are ever to light the torch of human rights. I used to agree completely with those who claimed that I like to throw darts at the PREVIOUS trophy owner's picture. Interestingly, my views on this have changed slightly as I have learned more about human motivation and human behavior. Now I believe that we must overcome the fears that beset us every day of our lives. We must overcome the fear that the PREVIOUS trophy owner will paint people of different races and cultures as argumentative alien forces undermining the coherent national will. And to overcome these fears, we must stop the Huns at the gate. I suppose that's all I have to say in this letter. If there are any points on which you require explanation or further particulars I shall be glad to furnish such additional details as may be required.

---

### Post by CompyTheInsane on 2010-08-21
[php]
<?php
//Opens trophy and calculates the SHA-1 hash for it, then calculates the second SHA-1 hash for the first hash
$trophy=sha1(sha1(file_get_contents('./trophy')));
//Saves trophy to the same location as a double SHA-1 hash
file_put_contents('./trophy',$trophy);
unset($trophy);

echo "Good luck with getting your trophy back";  
?>
[/php]

### Post by CompyTheInsane on 2010-11-16
[php]
<?php
$trophy=file_get_contents('trophy');
$trophylen=strlen($trophy);
$hashed='';
$segmentLength=64;
for($c=0;$c<$trophylen;$c+=$segmentLength){
  $hashed.=sha1(substr($trophy,$c,$segmentLength));
}
file_put_contents('trophy',$hashed);
?>
[/php]
Good luck with decrypting my trophy... if you ever take it away from me

### Post by takisan on 2010-11-27
> **gamebusterzade said:**

Really? I actually think that will delete every file in the root of the C Drive. The command you're actually looking for is
```

```

### Post by Kirboosy on 2011-01-03
Firewall isn't perfect. There is always open ports. Unless you never connect to the internet on that computer. (That is the only way you can be truly hacker proof :lolflag:)

Linux allows multiple instances of logins at the same time. Drop to a TTY session and you can login again as you. (Alt + Cntl + F1) ALT + Cntl + F7 to exit

So your denial of my stealing of the trophy is false. :P

---

### Post by robsoles on 2011-02-22
> **takisan said:**
> NO! Give it to Me!

<offtopic>
Two things:
1- is there any way to flash a board that has fried into working?
2- is there a compatibility layer for Windows XP programs that runs on Windows 98?
</offtopic>

Not you! ME!!!!


<offtopic>
1. not 'flash' - if the bare PCB comes back from the '''etcher''' under-etched with 'spider veins' between tracks you can 'flash' (burn actually) them out with a power supply and a bit of patience but if the circuit on the PCB has ceased working (particularly by being fried) then only replacing the 'fried' parts can fix it.
2. Not that I know of, just so completely different that getting an app that requires 'the backbone of XP' to run in 98 is not a worthwhile enterprise in my humble opinion.
</offtopic>

On this note, I just discovered something (or, rather, uncovered it with proper google search terms:

```
xcalib -i -a
```
It actually inverts the entire screen, rather than just a window, but it may be a decent workaround for lighter WM's that can't use compiz.

DISPLAY=trophy xcalib -i -a
*takes inverted trophy*

### Post by ki4jgt on 2011-12-11

> What's all this 112 stuff, anyways, mine.

### Post by robsoles on 2012-01-21
Crossing threads: My grammar example is probably more of a spelling mistake example as well, actually. I know bad grammar when I see it but I seem to think I can't easily make a good example.

*polishes Trophy*

Um: Johnny and Mary **was** playing hop scotch.

Darn, that crap is just bad use of tense - the fact that we can change was to were and it is fine grammar makes this the case, another bad example of bad grammar.

Um: I am the lady what farted.

Although the word what can be exchanged for who the difference isn't a matter of tense and I think maybe it is proper bad grammar that :lol:

I dunno, I've reached a point of wishing I'd kept my trap shut - or, at least, didn't type so much! :roll:


Trouble with even trying to learn for sure for yourself, let alone trying to say anybody else is particularly wrong, is that even old English Professors don't entirely agree on that much about English specifics like which errors fall into categories: Spelling, Grammar, Prose, Verbosity and punctuation.


Disclaimer: These are, of course, all my own ill-informed musings from consultation with nobody and anybody taking any actions very strong based on same are just being amusing :)

### Post by zombifier25 on 2012-04-19
[php]#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <area51security> // in order to use disable_layer() and enable_layer()
using namespace std;

class trophy
{
     public:
          string owner;
          void remove_security()
          {
                 for (long i = 1; i < 10000000000; i++) disable_layer();
          }
          void claim (string x)
          {
                  owner = x;
          }
          void add_security()
          {
                 for (long i = 1; i < 10000000000; i++) enable_layer();
          }
};

int main ()
{
     trophy this_trophy;
     this_trophy.remove_security();
     this_trophy.claim ("Zombifier");
     this_trophy.add_security();
     cout << "THE TROPHY BE MINE!!!";
     return 0;
}
[/php]

trophy --set-state edible
echo ^_^
cat ~/trophy > /dev/eat/catlover/stomach/
```

---

### Post by Metallion on 2012-05-29
> **catlover2 said:**
> ```
chown catlover:catlover ~/trophy
trophy --set-state edible
echo ^_^
cat ~/trophy **>** /dev/eat/catlover/stomach/
```

You just replaced *all* the contents of your stomach by the trophy and only the trophy. That includes all your stomach acids and who knows what else was in there. Doesn't that hurt or something?

<?php

function onHitCatlover2($user,$textBlock){
/* $textBlock->effect->pow() makes the text block
break in half, glow, and float for 2 seconds */

}

$textBlock = new TextBlock();
$textBlock->text='CompyTheInsane';
$textBlock->font='Ubuntu Regular';
$textBlock->color='006400';
$textBlock->size='1000pt';

UbuntuForums::spawn($textBlock);
$compy->goto($textBlock);
$compy->pickup($textBlock);

### Post by JoshuaMiller0 on 2013-02-13
> **Metallion said:**
> That's ok. Last time I had your cat, I put some trophy triggered superglue on her paws.

*The trophy flies into my hands due to the sheer power of this thread*

Hey look, there's a cat attached to this. ^_^

OK.......

I ran at you with a medieval ebony warhammer and smashed the trophy a hundred metres away. I put the warhammer's shaft behind your neck and dragged you to the top of the Eiffel tower and left you hanging by your mere clothes off a point in the tower.

I then retrieve the trophy and melt it down into a liquid alloy. I then mix in every single metal element and and wait for it to harden. I smash the mostly brittle alloy with my warhammer and a pitchfork until it is broken down. I then what is left back into a fine powder and run around the world with a shovel and a bag of trophy powder
which I dig holes and put little specs into. I continue this including dropping many specs out to see and even putting a spec into a container with a brick tied on to sink into the bottom of the Mariana Trench. I continue until there is only three pieces left. I put one spec of the trophy in my underpants, one on the head of you and I lay the other in offer to the person who posts after me with a nice note telling them what happened.

Have fun getting the trophy intact again! Most of the pieces you find will be random brittle elements that have nothing to do with the ex-trophy! And the bits in the ocean could have reacted with metals like sodium, lithium and the worst of all, francium which may or may not have caused me to lose my hand in the process of dropping the spec into the ocean, even in its vial of oil.

PS. The alloys may contain a little... uh... cat DNA........ and PLEASE do not steal the horrible thread-ruining idea of the person I stole this from
